sanskrit: मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम्। व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनः पुनस्त्वं तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य॥
transliteration: mā te vyathā mā ca vimūḍha-bhāvo dṛṣṭvā rūpaṃ ghoram īdṛṅ-mam-edaṃ | vyapeta-bhīḥ prīta-manaḥ punas tvaṃ tad eva me rūpam idaṃ prapaśya ||
literal_meaning: Let there be no agitation for you, nor bewilderment, having seen this terrible form of Mine. Free from fear, with a pleased mind, again you see that very form of Mine.
contextual_meaning: After witnessing the overwhelming cosmic form, Arjuna is reassured and guided back to a state of calm; Krishna re-establishes psychological safety and offers a relational form that supports loving connection rather than existential dread.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic soothing; transition from hyper-arousal to secure base activation.
trigger_scenario: A patient re-experiences dissociative flashbacks of ICU trauma; therapist gently grounds them, then invites visualization of a comforting inner figure to restore felt safety.
anchor_text: Return to calm; the beloved form always waits beyond awe.
